searchLedger.hypothesis: Production socket/database contact is isolated in the launcher orphan-reaping tests

searchActions.result: createWorkspace builds temp db/socket directories at lines 128-131, copies launcher/proxy fixtures into the temp workspace at lines 123-127, and spawnLauncher pins MK_SKILL_ADVISOR_DB_DIR plus SPECKIT_IPC_SOCKET_DIR to those temp paths at lines 150-159.

searchLedger.rationale: The tests do not bind the production socket path or use the production skill-advisor database for the reviewed launcher scenarios.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Sandbox-critical env vars and fixture paths are temp-scoped for the reviewed tests.

searchLedger.hypothesis: Second-client bridge test does assert end-to-end message flow

searchActions.result: The test writes an initialize request to the second launcher's stdin at lines 316-326, waits for stdout close at line 328, and asserts stdout contains the fixture daemon serverInfo name warm-advisor at line 334.

searchLedger.rationale: This is not merely socket-open coverage; it proves a client frame traverses the lease-holder session proxy and a daemon response reaches stdout.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The asserted stdout response is end-to-end evidence for this specific test.

title: Stale-lease test does not exercise the new owner-lease double-writer risk
evidence: The test seeds only workspace.leaseFilePath at lines 342-343, starts exactly one launcher at line 345, and never creates a stale .skill-advisor-owner.json. The new owner-lease reclaim branch in .opencode/bin/mk-skill-advisor-launcher.cjs uses exclusive creation only when no owner lease exists (lines 430-439), but overwrites a stale existing owner lease with writeOwnerLeaseFile at line 444.
rationale: This does not prove the single-writer invariant under the adversarial case that matters for the new code: two launchers concurrently classify the same owner lease as stale and both race through the non-exclusive overwrite path. A regression allowing two live writers could pass the current stale launcher-lease test.

title: Warm-daemon adoption test proves no respawn, but not that bridged traffic works
evidence: The test starts the launcher at line 396, waits for stdout close at line 397, and asserts stderr/no-child/no-launcher state at lines 400-405. It never writes a JSON-RPC client frame or asserts a response from the warm daemon. The bridge log is emitted before bridgeToSocket is awaited in .opencode/bin/lib/launcher-ipc-bridge.cjs:426-431.
rationale: A no-op or broken session-proxy bridge after a successful retry probe could still emit the bridge log, avoid spawning the stub daemon, leave childPidFile absent, and pass this test. The separate second-client test covers end-to-end flow for the live owner-lease path, but not this failed-first-probe adoption path.

title: Implementation summary claims completion while remaining scaffold text
evidence: Metadata says Completed 2026-06-11 and Level 2 at lines 41-43, but the document still contains template placeholders such as the opening hook at line 59, feature description at lines 61-64, delivery placeholder at line 86, verification placeholder at lines 110-112, and limitation placeholder at line 125.
rationale: The doc is not an implementation summary for the commit under review and cannot support release or review claims. It also conflicts with spec.md, which is still draft scaffold content rather than accepted requirements.

title: New spec docs contain banned ephemeral labels in comments
evidence: spec.md has an HTML comment listing REQ-003 through REQ-008 at lines 169-175. implementation-summary.md also has an HTML comment with a spec path reference at line 57.
rationale: The review instruction explicitly requested grepping changed code/comments for banned ephemeral labels, including spec paths and REQ-style ids. These are comments in newly added docs and should be removed or replaced with durable content.

title: Dead-pid fixture has a low-probability PID reuse race
evidence: createDeadPid spawns a process, kills it, waits for exit, and returns the now-dead pid at lines 200-210. The stale-lease test later relies on that pid being dead when the launcher probes it at lines 342-347.
rationale: If the OS reuses the pid before the launcher checks liveness, the fixture can misclassify the stale lease as live. This is unlikely but nondeterministic; a fake lease classifier or impossible pid strategy would be more stable.
